creator: Marina Temkin
title: (No AI) How Justin Ernest invested nearly $500M into hot startups without a traditional VC fund
dc:creator: Marina Temkin
content: Instead of spending a year raising a formal venture fund, the Sabertooth VC founder used a captive network of LPs to invest in startups like Anthropic, Anduril, and SpaceX.

title: (No AI) I tried Siri AI, and so far it actually works
author: Allison Johnson
content:
![Photo of new Siri icon on iPhone](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/DSC03775_processed.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Siri, are you there?﻿

Parents want one thing, and one thing only, out of AI: to add a list of soccer games or "spirit week" theme days from an email or a poorly formatted flyer [onto their calendar in one shot](https://www.theverge.com/tech/696885/ios-26-visual-intelligence-calendar-screenshot-gemini-assistant). And I have good news for parents with iPhones - the new Siri can finally do this.

After [stumbling through its first launch](https://www.theverge.com/tech/944245/apple-wwdc-2026-ai-siri-gemini) of an AI-imbued Siri, Apple is trying again. [The newly upgraded Siri AI](https://www.theverge.com/tech/942416/apple-siri-ai-update-wwdc) can chat with you about what might be killing the roses in your yard, put together a shopping list for the hardware store, and set a reminder to lay down some compost in that flower bed. It can reference information in your email and calendar to make its recommenda …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/947432/siri-ai-apple-intelligence-ios-27-wwdc)

Siri, are you there?﻿	

Parents want one thing, and one thing only, out of AI: to add a list of soccer games or "spirit week" theme days from an email or a poorly formatted flyer onto their calendar in one shot. And I have good news for parents with iPhones - the new Siri can finally do this.
After stumbling through its first launch of an AI-imbued Siri, Apple is trying again. It can reference information in your email and calendar to make its recommenda …

title: (No AI) GM thinks EVs can help offset AI’s energy suck with vehicle-to-grid tech
author: Andrew J. Hawkins
content:
![GM vehicle to grid](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/GM-Energy-home-energy-system-2.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

At an event in San Francisco today, General Motors made a series of announcements around EV batteries, energy storage, and grid resiliency in the face of [growing electricity demand from AI data centers](https://www.theverge.com/news/845831/ai-chips-data-center-power-water). The automaker announced that it would be activating new vehicle-to-grid capabilities for its current EV and home energy customers. It's releasing a new commercial energy storage system strategy, anchored by newly developed sodium-ion batteries for industrial-scale grid applications. And it's launching a new feature for EV owners that it says will help simplify public charging.

Right now, millions of EVs are sitting idly in driveways across …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/transportation/946820/gm-energy-ev-v2g-storage-sodium-ion)

creator: Katie Drummond
title: (No AI) Alex Vindman Survived Trump’s Retaliation Machine. Now He’s Running for Senate
dc:creator: Katie Drummond
content: In 2019, Alex Vindman testified during President Trump’s first impeachment trial–a decision that ended his military career. Now he wants to challenge the president from the halls of Congress.
contentSnippet: In 2019, Alex Vindman testified during President Trump’s first impeachment trial–a decision that ended his military career. Now he wants to challenge the president from the halls of Congress.
